prompt: Implement `groupAnagrams(strs: string[]): string[][]` that groups anagrams together. Order of groups and order within each group does not matter.
canonicalSolution: export function groupAnagrams(strs: string[]): string[][] { const map = new Map<string, string[]>(); for (const s of strs) { const key = s.split('').sort().join(''); const group = map.get(key) ?? []; group.push(s); map.set(key, group); } return [...map.values()]; }
test: const r1 = groupAnagrams(['eat','tea','tan','ate','nat','bat']); assert(r1.length===3); const sorted1 = r1.map(g=>g.slice().sort().join(',')).sort(); assert(sorted1[0]==='ate,eat,tea'); assert(sorted1[1]==='bat'); assert(sorted1[2]==='nat,tan'); const r2 = groupAnagrams(['']); assert(r2.length===1 && r2[0]!.length===1); const r3 = groupAnagrams(['a']); assert(r3.length===1 && r3[0]![0]==='a');
